To the women who knew us before we knew ourselves. We spent the afternoon hosting our moms at the office, trading childhood stories, and realizing that every bit of our success started with them. It was a beautiful reminder that while we’re busy building careers, they’re the ones who built the foundation. 💙

Meet Manvi,

She’s not an engineer but she still built a tech tool with AI that’s now widely used across Cars24.

Because what she chose to do was simple - she noticed a problem and stayed with it long enough to figure out a solution. 

Everything began from a pattern she kept seeing internally on how good work kept getting lost.

The dashboards, reports, docs - all being created faster than ever with AI across teams, but shared in ways that didn’t last. On slack threads & random folders.

So she built a simple tool: HTMLHub. Upload a file, get a link, share it with context, keep it accessible. 

And 3 months later:
1000+ people using it internally
800+ uploads every month 

But what stands out isn’t just the adoption. It’s how one person, without a traditional tech background, saw a problem up close and built something to solve it

She showed us what really “Everyone is a builder” means in practice and we are proud of her.

Read her full story in the blog, link in the first comment below 👇

The Founders Awards are not about tenure. Not about titles. Not about who was in the room when the big decisions were made. They are about who made the big decisions feel possible, through clarity when things were murky, through courage when things were hard, through consistency when it would have been easier to look away.

HYROX INDIA

In 2024, India lost 1,80,000 people on its roads. One every three minutes. 66% of them were between 18 and 34. Nearly 10,000 were school students.

Seven in ten of these deaths were caused by overspeeding. An individual's choice, not a necessity.

This reality compelled us to start Crashfree India, because as an enterprise that puts as many cars on Indian roads as we do, we cannot look away from what happens on them. And the more we have worked on this, the clearer one thing has become: infrastructure alone will not fix this.

What we need is a cultural and mindset shift. In how we think about speed, about the road, and about the lives around us every time we drive.

Today, we are proud to welcome MS Dhoni as Goodwill Ambassador for Crashfree India, to strengthen and amplify this commitment.

Having someone like him, gives it exactly the kind of priority it deserves. Because as Dhoni says, we need to stop calling reckless driving a skill. If we all drive with a sense of responsibility, thousands of lives will be saved.

With him on board, the intention is to shift behaviour at scale, make responsibility visible, and make safe driving something people take pride in.

Welcome, MS.

We spoke to a 22 year old in Delhi who lost both his parents in a car crash. It has been 1.5 years and he is still waiting for compensation. He is now overburdened by debt and cannot afford to pay his college fees.

His story is just one of the many we heard while speaking to families who lost their loved ones in a crash, only to be hurt by the very system that was built to protect them.

India has comprehensive road crash compensation laws. But for victims, seeing that money means navigating a process that is fragmented, slow, and difficult to understand at a time when you are least equipped to deal with it. And this is possibly why over Rs 80,000 crore in claims remain unpaid across the country.

While there are countless infrastructure initiatives focused on preventing crashes, the post-crash experience rarely gets the same attention. That gap is exactly what Crashfree India has spent the last six months trying to understand and address.

We mapped where the implementation of compensation policies falls short. We built multilingual legal awareness tools, visual explainers, and are in the process of developing AASHA, an AI-enabled chatbot that walks victims through their rights step by step in the language they understand. While also working with the Gurugram Police to proactively identify eligible hit-and-run cases and help families navigate claims they never knew they could make.

The report below captures this journey end to end. It documents the key bottlenecks in India's compensation process and lays the groundwork for the policy and institutional reforms needed to make justice accessible, timely and equitable for every victim.

Because compensation after a crash should not depend on awareness or luck. Period.

On December 14, 1983, a retired government officer in Delhi took delivery of a small white car. He didn't know he was holding a piece of history.

For decades, the average Indian family could only dream of owning a car. The Ambassador was too expensive. The Premier Padmini was a city thing. And then came the Maruti 800 — ₹47,500. Simple. Reliable. Small enough for city lanes. Big enough for a family of four and all their hope.

It wasn't just a car. It was the moment the middle class said: we belong here too.

Millions of Indians learned to drive in an 800. Got married, moved cities, drove parents to hospitals, taught their kids on empty Sunday roads — all in that same little machine.

Some of those cars are still running. And some of them come to us.

#Cars24 #Maruti800 #IndianCarHistory #Automotive #UsedCars

You are driving right now with an invention made for a man who couldn't turn his head.

Ray Harroun was racing at the first ever Indianapolis 500 in 1911. Every other driver had a mechanic sitting beside them, someone to watch the cars behind. Harroun didn't want the extra weight. So he bolted a small mirror to the front of his car. Angled it. And raced alone.

He won.

The rearview mirror wasn't born in a lab. Not by an engineer with a whiteboard. It was born from desperation, on a racetrack, at 100 miles per hour.

Every car you've ever driven has one. Every car ever sold on Cars24 has one. Some inventions are so simple they disappear into the background, until you realise someone had to be the first person to think of them.

#Cars24 #CarHistory #Automotive #DidYouKnow #IndianUsedCars

Something that moved us deeply 💙 

Mr. Savdhan, a farmer from Banaskantha, a small district in Gujarat, lost his son and daughter-in-law in the recent Ahmedabad plane crash. His son had always dreamt of gifting him a white Nexon.

Last week, Savdhan ji travelled over 200 km to our hub to finally bring that car home. Our team made sure it was done with utmost care, dignity, and respect.

There isn’t much to say after seeing something like that. But it reminded us why we exist, to do the right thing for people, especially when it matters most.

Here’s to bringing better drives, better lives to life, in every way that truly counts

It made us think about how rarely we pause to enjoy what we already have. Maybe we don’t need more reasons to celebrate, we just need to notice the ones right in front of us.
Kudos to our Finance and Legal team R&R winners, the ones who show up, step up, and raise the bar with their passion and teamwork. You inspire us to keep pushing forward, every day.

We often measure growth by output: targets met, products launched, metrics achieved. But behind every outcome is a much quieter variable: cognitive energy. Research shows that nearly one in three professionals globally experiences symptoms of burnout every year and that sustained mental fatigue can reduce productivity by up to 40%. Yet most of us spend more time managing deadlines than managing our minds.

At CARS24, we have started looking at mental health not as a response to stress, but as a core capability, the same way we look at decision-making, problem-solving or fitness. Mental fitness, like physical strength, is built through routine: small, consistent habits that help us stay balanced before pressure hits.

This World Mental Health Day, our conversations with our wellness coach Sukhmani Pal, Ph.D and our leaders Manoj Yadav, Neha Bhandari, Ankit Bhalla and Vedika Gulati were centered around that idea how clarity, recovery and self-awareness drive better performance than intensity alone. The takeaway was simple but profound: balance isn’t a state you find, it’s a system you build.

When people learn to pause with purpose, to rest, reflect and reset, they don’t slow down progress. They make it sustainable.

Every home has a heartbeat. At CARS24, it’s Sandy Bhaiya.

When CARS24 began in 2015, Sandy bhaiya was there , brewing tea for four or five people, including our founders. Today, he serves 1000+ Autonauts every single day.

From chole kulche to protein shakes, you name it, and Sandy Bhaiya has it. His cafe recently got a fresh revamp - new menu, new look, and even new packaging that reflects our values and spreads a little more love with every meal.

After years of dedication (and hardly ever taking a day off), Sandy Bhaiya’s FSSAI license has been approved recently, a small but meaningful step that recognizes his passion.

Because behind every great workplace, there are people like Sandy Bhaiya, the ones who make it feel like home.
